gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/347)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/347)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ بِأَبِي أَنْتَ وَ أُمِّي إِنِّي جَعَلْتُ عَلَى نَفْسِي مَشْياً إِلَى بَيْتِ اللَّهِ قَالَ كَفِّرْ يَمِينَكَ فَإِنَّمَا جَعَلْتَ عَلَى نَفْسِكَ يَمِيناً وَ مَا جَعَلْتَهُ لِلَّهِ فَفِ بِهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Al Sindy Bin Muhammad, from Safwan Al Jammal,

Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘May my father be (sacrificed) for you<sup>asws</sup> and my mother! I made it upon myself for walking to the House of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Expiate your vow, for rather you have made an oath upon yourself, so whatever you have made it to be for the Sake of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, so fulfill with it’.
en.sarwar: 18. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Sindiy ibn Muhammad from Safwan al-Jammal who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, I have made a vow and commitment to walk to the House of Allah. He (the Imam) said, ‘You must pay expiation for your oath, because you have made it on yourself and you have not made for Allah; you must then fulfill.’”
